source: Liberty Investigates; London Now; Iain Gould solicitors; Doughty Street Chambers; Parliamentary Research Briefings; ICO audit reports
notes: Retrospective Facial Recognition (RFR) — police apply FR software to still images or CCTV footage after an incident to identify suspects. Distinct from Live LFR (real-time scanning) and Operator-Initiated FR (handheld on-demand). Different legal framework: governed by Data Protection Act 2018 and common law, not the specific LFR safeguards. Met Police increased RFR searches by 700% in 2024, from 3,030 to 24,677 searches. Searches draw on Police National Database (19+ million custody images).
technology_note: Three types of police FR technology in use in London: (1) LFR — fixed/mobile cameras scan faces against a watchlist in real time; (2) RFR — existing footage submitted to FR software after the fact; (3) OIFR — handheld device captures a face on demand for immediate matching. See data/deployments/met-police-lfr.json for LFR, data/deployments/btp-lfr.json for BTP LFR, and agents/README.md for OIFR documentation.

incidents.legal_outcome: Acquitted by magistrates — alibi confirmed (running AA meeting 10 miles from IKEA at time of theft)
incidents.false_positive_notes: RFR system mismatched McMahon to the shoplifting suspect. Alibi independently verified. Met updated RFR protocols following the case.
incidents.notes: Reported April 2026. Original Gmail research thread: news-002 in data/news/gmail-research-threads.json. Arrest location (Harlesden) is in news-002; footage location (IKEA Greenwich) and acquittal details from article content. This incident is a Retrospective FR case, NOT a Live LFR deployment — important distinction for map layer categorisation.

incidents.legal_outcome: Wrongful arrest — charges dropped; subject pursuing compensation
incidents.false_positive_notes: Officers matched Choudhury on generic racial similarities (Asian appearance), ignoring obvious physical disparities including 4+ years of aging difference. 97% error cited. Liberty Investigates documented racial bias in the match.
incidents.notes: Not a London/Met incident — Thames Valley Police, Milton Keynes. Included as documented UK RFR false-positive case with named victim and legal representation. Represented by Iain Gould solicitors and Doughty Street Chambers. Supports comparative analysis of RFR racial bias patterns across forces.

incidents.legal_outcome: Wrongful arrest — damages paid to victim
incidents.false_positive_notes: Victim appeared 32nd in FR system's suggested match list. Officers selected the match despite low confidence ranking.
incidents.notes: South Wales Police, not Met — included for comparative context. South Wales is also the force in the Bridges [2020] landmark LFR case. ICO audited SWP and Gwent Police RFR practices Aug 2025, finding previously unknown historical bias in the Police National Database algorithm.

incidents.footage_location_name: Multiple locations across England — August 2024 riots
incidents.original_offence: Public order offences (riots following Southport stabbings)
incidents.legal_outcome: Multiple convictions — mix of correct IDs and potential false positives not yet individually documented
incidents.notes: Mass RFR deployment following August 2024 UK riots triggered by Southport stabbings. Police used CCTV footage, video doorbells, and social media content processed through FR systems to identify participants, including masked individuals. Guardian reported even masked rioters could be tracked. Collective record — individual cases not yet individually documented in public sources. Related to news-021 in gmail-research-threads.json.

ico_audit_2025.findings: Medium and low priority compliance recommendations; previously unknown historical bias discovered in Police National Database algorithm

operator_initiated_fr.note: A third type — OIFR — is being trialled by Met Police from Feb 2026. Handheld mobile app (~100 devices, £763,000) captures face on demand for immediate matching. Not retrospective; not fixed-camera LFR. Powered by NEC Neoface software.
